title: Integrative deep learning of spatial multi-omics with SWITCH
data_sources: E13 mouse embryo spatial ATAC-RNA-seq (Li et al. 2023)
P22 mouse brain spatial CUT&TAG-RNA-seq H3K27ac (Li et al. 2023)
P22 mouse brain spatial CUT&TAG-RNA-seq H3K4me3 (Li et al. 2023)
P21 mouse brain spatial RNA-ATAC-seq (Li et al. 2023)
MISAR-seq mouse embryo unpaired sections (Liao et al. 2023)
Adult mouse brain Stereo-seq (Chen et al. 2022)
Mouse brain single-nucleus ATAC-seq (Zhu et al. 2021)

data_scope.tissue_types: mouse embryo (E13, P21, P22), adult mouse brain coronal section
data_scope.omics_modalities: spatial transcriptomics (RNA), spatial chromatin accessibility (ATAC), histone modification (CUT&TAG: H3K27ac, H3K4me3), single-nucleus ATAC-seq
data_scope.note: Exact spot counts and feature dimensions per dataset are in Supplementary Table 2; not reported in main text

core_conclusion: SWITCH, a deep generative model with a cycle-mapping mechanism and iterative pseudo-pair refinement, integrates unpaired spatial multi-omics data and performs reliable cross-modal imputation without requiring paired training data. It outperforms nine competing methods by up to 59% in overall integration score and 249% in FOSKNN, achieves higher-resolution brain cortical layer delineation, provides per-spot uncertainty estimates, and enables downstream differential analysis, trajectory inference, and gene regulatory network reconstruction from single-modality spatial data.
why_it_matters: Breaks the co-profiling cost barrier by enabling reliable multi-omics inference from single-modality data; first spatially-aware unpaired integration framework; provides built-in uncertainty quantification missing from competing tools; extends to cross-technology scenarios (spatial transcriptomics + dissociated single-nucleus ATAC-seq); fully open and reproducible.
why_nature: Principled algorithmic novelty (cycle-mapping harmonization, iterative pseudo-pair refinement) applicable to future spatial platforms; rigorous benchmarking against nine methods with multiple quantitative metrics; enables new biology (trajectory inference, GRN discovery) from cheaper single-modality data; addresses pressing community need as co-profiling costs remain prohibitive.
added_value_to_my_research: Cycle-mapping fusion framework for unpaired spatiotemporal health data (mobile phone + social media + remote sensing) without co-observation requirement
SWITCH's spatially-weighted GAT encoder as a pre-trainable spatial feature backbone for GeoAI models, analogous to geospatial foundation model fine-tuning
Uncertainty-aware spatial imputation of missing healthcare facility supply data, propagating confidence bounds into R2SFCA accessibility scores
Two-stage adversarial alignment + Moran's I quality metric adapted to multi-source urban neighborhood delineation and community detection
Physics-informed spatial pathway inference for place-health cascades, guided by a domain-knowledge graph encoding known determinant-outcome relationships

related.title: Spatial multi-omics of human skin reveals KRAS and inflammatory responses to spaceflight
related.relation: applied spatial multi-omics demonstrating biological questions SWITCH's integration targets

related.title: Scaling and quantization of a foundational deep learning model for network biology
related.relation: foundation model pre-training for biological data, analogous strategy to SWITCH's encoder pre-training extension

related.title: AUTOENCODIX: a generalized and versatile framework to train and evaluate autoencoders for biological representation learning and beyond
related.relation: autoencoder benchmarking framework for biological omics, complementary to SWITCH's variational architecture

related.title: Cell neighborhood topology directs rare cell population identification
related.relation: spatial cell neighborhood topology methods complementary to SWITCH's domain delineation

related.title: HESpotEx: a dual-stream deep learning framework for spot-level gene expression prediction from histological images
related.relation: spot-level cross-modal prediction (histology to gene expression) directly analogous to SWITCH's cross-modal translation task

related.title: Deep learning training dynamics analysis for single-cell data
related.relation: training dynamics and annotation quality assessment for single-cell deep learning, methodologically related to SWITCH's iterative pseudo-pair refinement strategy

figures.caption: Architecture of SWITCH framework — spatial proximity graphs, GAT encoders, adversarial alignment guided by knowledge graph, pseudo-pair refinement, and cycle-mapping mechanism
Benchmarking SWITCH integration performance against nine methods on E13 mouse embryo and P22 mouse brain datasets (omics mixing, biological conservation, Moran's I, ICC, FOSCTTM, FOSKNN)
Cross-modal imputation and uncertainty estimation — Pearson correlations, uncertainty-error relationship, differential analysis validation for eye domain (Vax2, Six3os1)
Cross-modal imputation in unpaired mouse embryo data enabling cortical developmental trajectory inference, peak-gene links, and Neurod1-centered GRN reconstruction

Integration of Stereo-seq ST and single-nucleus ATAC-seq for adult mouse brain — inferred spatial chromatin accessibility and TF activity scores (Mef2c, Sox10, Egr3, Fos) validated against Allen Brain Atlas ISH
